title: viola × maxima
intro: Viola × maxima, a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Viola sororia and Viola sororia var. sororia, is a perennial species of the viola genus. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Viola × maxima include its potential to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce stress. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, tincture, or decoction, with common preparations including teas, salves, and topical creams.
